name: Kijun Equilibrium Mean Reversion
edge_hypothesis: Kijun-sen (IKS_26) is the 26-period midpoint of the high-low range — a natural equilibrium price. In ranging markets (ADX < 25), price oscillates around this equilibrium and deviations should revert. No Ichimoku indicator has EVER appeared in any strategy spec. By combining Kijun distance with RSI confirmation, we catch mean-reversion entries at statistically significant displacements from equilibrium. This is distinct from Bollinger/Donchian approaches because Kijun adapts to the midpoint of the actual range, not a statistical band or fixed lookback channel.

variants.name: kijun_mr_1h_8to1
variants.edge_hypothesis: 1h ETH ranging mean-reversion. Price below Kijun equilibrium + RSI oversold + low ADX = classic ranging snap-back setup. 8:1 R:R is the proven ACCEPT floor. 3 conditions should fire regularly in ranging ETH (Kijun displacement + RSI < 35 co-occur naturally at swing lows).

variants.name: kijun_mr_4h_10to1
variants.edge_hypothesis: 4h Kijun displacement is rarer but higher conviction — a 4h close below the 26-bar midpoint with RSI < 35 represents a significant deviation. Wider 2.0x ATR stop accommodates 4h noise. 10:1 R:R captures the larger expected reversion move on higher timeframe.
